verses.text: The LORD at Sinai: when ye come to the land, the land shall keep a sabbath to the LORD. Six years sow field, prune vineyard. The seventh — a sabbath of rest; sow not, reap not. What grows of itself feeds thee, servant, stranger, cattle, beast in the land. Guidance: Even the soil gets sabbath. Every seventh year — no planting, no reaping. The earth rests, the poor and animals eat the volunteers. God writes into the calendar what no farmer would write for himself.

Seven sabbaths of years — forty-nine. The trumpet sounds on Atonement Day of the fiftieth year — proclaim liberty throughout the land. Jubilee — every man returns to his possession, his family. Sow not, reap not; eat what the land yields. Guidance: A debt-reset every fifty years. The jubilee trumpet sounds on Atonement Day — forgiveness in heaven becomes release on earth. Lincoln engraved Lev 25:10 on the Liberty Bell; the verse is still ringing.

In the Jubilee return to your possession. If thou sell to thy neighbor or buy — count years from Jubilee; price by years of crops remaining. More years — higher price; fewer — lower. Oppress not one another; fear thy God. Guidance: Real estate isn't permanent; it's a lease until the next Jubilee. Price scales with years of harvest left. No one can be priced out forever — God built reset into the market.

Do My statutes — dwell safely in the land. The land shall yield her fruit, ye shall eat your fill. If ye say: what shall we eat the seventh year? I will command My blessing in the sixth — fruit for three years. Guidance: The question is reasonable — and God answers it before they ask. The sixth year's harvest will feed years six, seven, and eight. God's economy never starves His people on the day they obey.

The land shall not be sold for ever — the land is Mine; ye are strangers and sojourners with Me. If thy brother sells his possession — his kinsman redeems. If he later prospers — restore the overplus. If unable, abide till Jubilee. Guidance: The land is God's; Israel only holds it on loan. A kinsman-redeemer can buy back a lost field. Ruth and Boaz live in this very law. Christ is called our kinsman-redeemer — He buys back what we lost.

A house in a walled city — redeem within a year; not redeemed, established to the buyer. A village house without walls — counted with the field, returns in Jubilee. Levite houses — may redeem always; their cities never sold. Field of their suburbs — never sold; perpetual possession. Guidance: Different rules for urban, village, and Levite property. God's law is not one-size — context matters. But Levites, who own no farmland, are protected forever.

If thy brother be waxen poor — relieve him, though a stranger or sojourner. Take no usury or increase; fear thy God. Give not money on usury, nor victuals for increase. I am the LORD who brought you from Egypt to give you Canaan. Guidance: No interest from a poor brother. The free gift of Canaan becomes the model: as God gave you a land freely, give your brother loans freely. Lending without profit is gospel economics.

Brother sold for poverty — not bondservant. As hired servant; serve till Jubilee. They are My servants whom I brought from Egypt. Rule not with rigour. Bondmen and bondmaids may be of heathen round about — bondmen forever. But over brethren of Israel rule not with rigour. Guidance: A poor Israelite stays free even when in service — only foreigners could be enslaved permanently. The law isn't an abolition manifesto, but it sets Israel apart: brothers cannot be owned.

If a stranger waxen rich and thy brother poor sold to him — be redeemed. Uncle, kinsman may redeem; or himself. Reckon to Jubilee; price by years. Not rule rigorously. If not redeemed, go out in Jubilee. For Israelites are My servants — I brought from Egypt: I am the LORD your God. Guidance: Even if a Hebrew gets sold to a foreigner, he can be bought back — by family or by himself. Jubilee is the absolute backstop. No one stays enslaved among God's people.
